body.value: # Remigius episcopus habet. I. maneriolum cum. I. carucata in contiguum ciuitateti Lincolia. ae cum saca et soca et cum Thol et Theim. et super III. mansiiones similiter. et super. II. aecclesias. et super. LXXVIII. mansiones similiter. praeter geldum regis quod dant cum burgensibus. Ex his sunt. XX. mansiones Wastae. De tribus superioribus mansionibus est. I. quieta ab omnibus rebus.

Bishop Remigius has 1 small manor [* `Willingthorpe' *] with 1 carucate adjacent to the City of LINCOLN, with full jurisdiction and market rights; likewise in respect of 3 messuages, 2 churches and 78 messuages except for the King's tax which they pay with the burgesses. Of these, 20 messuages are unoccupied. Of the 3 messuages above, 1 is exempt from everything, but 2 are taxed with the burgesses.

In campis Lincoliae extra sunt XII. carucatae terrae et dimidia. praeter carucatam episcopi ciuitatis. De hac terra habent Rex et comes. VIII. carucatas in dominio. Ex his dedit unam rex Willelmus cuidam Ulchel pro una naui quam ab eo emit. Ille uero qui nauim uendidit mortuus. est. et hanc carucatam terre nullus habet nisi rege concedente. Praeter has. VIII. carucatas. habet rex et comes CC et XXX. I. acras terrae arabilis inland. et C. acras prati.

In the fields of LINCOLN outside the City there are 12 1/2 carucates of land, besides the carucate of the Bishop of the City. Of this land the King and the Earl have 8 carucates in lordship. King William gave one of them to Ulfkil for a ship which he bought from him. But the man who sold the ship is dead and no one has this carucate of land except with the King's consent. Besides these 8 carucates the King and the Earl have 231 acres of arable land, {inland}, and 100 acres of meadow.
# De reliqua terra idest. IIII. carucatae et dimidia: T. R. E. habuit Ulf. I. carucatam. nunc habet filius eius Sortebrand.
Of the remaining land, that is 4 1/2 carucates, before 1066 Ulf [* Fenman *] had 1 carucate, now his son Swartbrand has it. ^[Lincoln]^

# Aliam carucatam. T. R. E. habuerunt Siuuard presbyter et Outi. et VI. acras terrae quas tenet Uluiet presbyter. Nunc habet Alfnod medietatem huius carucatae. et Norman filius Siuuard presbyteri alteram medietatem. Hanc uero praedictam medietatem istius terre. et uxorem Siuuardi presbyteri inuasit Unlof presbyter dum erat iN saisitione regis propter. XL. solidos quos ipsemet rex imposuerat super Siuuardum presbyterum.

Before 1066 Siward the priest and Auti had another carucate, and 6 acres of meadow, which Wulfgeat the priest holds. Now Alnoth has half of this carucate and Norman son of Siward the priest the other half. But Wulfnoth the priest sei
# Terciam carucatam habet Petrus de Ualongies. quam habuit Godricus T. R. E.
Peter of Valognes has the third carucate which Godric had before 1066. ^[Lincoln]^

# Quarta carucata adiacuit in aecclesia omnium sanctorum. T. R. E. et XII. toftes. et IIII. croftes. Hanc aecclesiam et terram aecclesiae et quicquid ad eam pertinuit. habuit Godricus filius Gareuinae. Sed eo facto monacho. abbas de Burg obtinet. Burgenses uero omnes Lincoliae dicunt quod iniuste habet. quia nec Gareuin nec Godricus filius eius nec ullus alius dare potuerunt extra ciuitatem nec extra parentes eorum. nisi concessu regis. Hanc aecclesiam et que ibi pertinet clamat Ernuin presbyter. . . hereditate Godrici consanguinei /sui.

The fourth carucate was attached to the church of All Saints before 1066, also 12 plots and 4 crofts. Godric son of Garwine had this church, the church's land and whatever belonged to it. But now that he has become a monk the Abbot of Peterborough holds them. But all the burgesses of LINCOLN say that he has them wrongfully, because neither Garwine nor Godric his son nor anyone else could grant them outside the City or outside their family except with the King's consent. This church and what belongs to it Ernwin the priest claims by inheritance from his kinsman Godric.
# Residuam dimidiam carucatam terrae habuit et habet SANCTA MARIA de Lincolia. in qua nunc est episcopatus.
St Mary's of Lincoln, where the Bishopric is now, had and has the remaining 1/2 carucate of land. ^[Lincoln]^

# Colsuen habet in Lincolia ciuitate. IIII. toftes de terra Cole nepotis sui. et extra ciuitatem habet XXX. VI. domos et II. aecclesias in quibus nichil adiacet. quas hospitauit in Wasta terra quam rex sibi dedit. et quae nunquam ante hospitata fuit. Modo habet rex omnes consuetudines ex eis.

Kolsveinn [* of Lincoln *] has in the City of LINCOLN 4 plots of his nephew Cola's land. Outside the City he has 36 houses and 2 churches to which nothing is attached, which he settled on waste land which the King gave him and which had never been settled before. Now the King has all the customary dues from them.
T. R. E. reddebat ciuitas Lincolia regi. XX. libras. et comiti. X. libras. Modo reddit. C. libras ad numerum. inter regem et comitem. Moneta uero. . . reddit. LXX. V. libras.
Before 1066 the City of LINCOLN paid £20 to the King and £10 to the Earl. Now it pays £100 at face value between the King and the Earl, but the mint pays £75.
Consuetudines regis et comitis in Sudlincolia: reddunt. XXVIII. libras.
The customary dues of the King and the Earl in South Lincoln[shire] pay £28. ^[Lincoln]^
In Norttreding consuetudines regis et comitis. reddunt. XX. IIII. libras.
In the North Riding [* of Lindsey *] the customary dues of the King and the Earl pay £24. ^[Lincoln]^
In Westreding consuetudines regis et comitis: reddunt. XII. libras.
In the West Riding [* of Lindsey *] the customary dues of the King and the Earl pay £12. ^[Lincoln]^
In Sudtreding consuetudines regis et comitis: reddunt. XV. libras.
In the South Riding [* of Lindsey *] the customary dues of the King and the Earl pay £15. ^[Lincoln]^

STANFORD BURGUM REGIS. dedit geldum T. R. E. pro. XII. hundrez et dimidio. In exercitu. et nauigio. et in Danegeld. Ibi fuerunt et sunt. VI. custodie. Quinque in Lincolescyre. et sexta in Hantunescyre. quae est ultra pontem. et tamen ipsa reddebat omnem consuetudinem cum aliis. praeter gablum et theloneum. quod abbas de Burg habebat et habet.

The King's Borough of STAMFORD paid tax before 1066 for 12 1/2 hundreds for the army, ship service and for Danegeld. There were and are six wards: five in Lincolnshire and the sixth in Northamptonshire - it is across the bridge; however, it paid every customary due with the other [wards] except for tribute and toll, which the Abbot of Peterborough had and has.
In his. V. custodiis T. R. E. fuerunt. CXL et I. mansiones. et dimidium molendinum. quae reddebant omnes consuetudines. Sed et modo totidem sunt. praeter. V. quae propter opus castri sunt wastae.

In these five wards before 1066 there were 141 messuages and 1/2 mill which paid all the customary dues; but now there are as many except for 5 which are unoccupied because of the construction of the castle. ^[Stamford]^

In his custodiis sunt LXVII. mansiones sochemanorum. qui habent terras suas in dominio. et qui petunt dominos ubi uolunt. super quos rex nichil aliud habet nisi emendationem foris facturae eorum. et heriete. et theloneum. et I. molinus de. XXX. solidis quod abstulit Eustacius de huntedune. Ilud erat unius sochemanni.

In these wards there are 77 messuages of Freemen who have their lands in lordship and seek lords where they will. The King has nothing over them except for a fine [consisting] of their forfeiture, heriot and toll. 1 mill at 30s which Eustace of Huntingdon took away; it was one of the Freemen's. ^[Stamford]^

In Stanford. T. R. E. erant. XII. Lagemanni. qui habebant infra domos suas sacam et socam. et super homines suos. praeter geld et heriete. et forisfacturam corporum suorum de XL. oris argenti. et preter Latronem. Hoc idem modo habent. Sed non sunt nisi nouem. Unus eorum habet XVII. mansiones sub se. et dimidium molinum. XV. solidorum. Alter. XIIII. cim mansiones. una ex his. est Wasta. Tercius. II. as mansiones. Quartus. II. as et dimidiam. Quintus. quinque Sextus. IIII. or Septimus. III. es Octauus. unam. Nonus. III. es sed Hugo musardus duas ei abstulit.

In STAMFORD before 1066 there were 12 lawmen who had full jurisdiction within their houses and over their men, except for tax, heriot, forfeiture involving their bodies at 40 {orae} of silver, and robbery. They also have this now, but they are only nine; one of them has 17 messuages under him and 1/2 mill, 15s; the second, 14 messuages - one of these is unoccupied; the third, 2 messuages; the fourth, 2 1/2; the fifth, 5; the sixth, 4; the seventh, 3; the eighth, 1; the ninth, 3, but Hugh Musard has taken 2 away from him.

In his custodiis adhuc sunt. XXII. mansiones. et II. aecclesiae cum. XII. acris terrae de. XIIII. solidis. quas habebat Ernuin presbyter. T. R. E. et Ezi habebat. I. mansionem. has. XX. III. mansiones habet modo Eudo dapifer. Super eas habebat rex omnem consuetudinem. modo non habet.

In these wards there are still 22 messuages and 2 churches with 12 acres of land at 14s which Ernwin the priest had before 1066; Edsi had 1 messuage. Eudo the steward now has these 23 messuages. The King had every customary due in respect of them, now he does not. ^[Stamford]^
In eadem uilla habuit Azor. VII. mansiones et dimidium molinum. T. R. E. modo habet Gunfridus de cioches. Ad has pertinent. LXX. acrae extra uillam.
Also in this town A

# Edded regina habuit. LXX. mansiones quae iacuerunt in Roteland cum omnibus consuetudinibus. sine panificis. Ad has adiacent II. carucatae terrae et dimidia. et I. caruca arans. et XLV. acrae prati extra uillam. Modo habet Rex. Willelmus. et ualet VI. libras. T. R. E. ualuit. IIII. libras.

Queen Edith had 70 messuages which lay in `Roteland', with all the customary dues, except the bakers'. Attached to them are 2 1/2 carucates of land and 1 plough, which is ploughing, and 45 acres of meadow outside the town. Now King William has them. Value £6; value before 1066 £4. ^[Stamford]^
# Abbas de Burg habuit et habet in Stanford X. mansiones pertinentes ad Lincolesyre. et I. molinum. XL. solidorum. et V. solidos de domibus et de VIII. /acris.
In STAMFORD the Abbot of Peterborough had and has 10 messuages which belong to Lincolnshire; 1 mill, 40s; 5s from houses and from 8 acres.
# Albertus unam aecclesiam Sancti Petri cum. II. abus mansionibus. et dimidiam carucatam terre quae iacet in Rotelande in hemeldune. Ualet. X. solidos.
Albert ^[of Lorraine]^ [has?] 1 church, St Peter's [* of Stamford *], with 2 messuages and 1/2 carucate of land which lies in `Roteland', in Hambleton. Value 10s. ^[Stamford]^
# T. R. E. dabat Stanford. XV. libras. modo dat ad firmam. L. libras. De omni consuetudine regis. modo dat. XXVIII. libras.
Before 1066 STAMFORD paid £15, now it pays £50 in revenue. It now pays £28 in respect of every customary due of the King's. List 3 [Torksey]

Hoc suburbium Torchesyg et Harduic Manerium ei contiguum habuit Eddid regina in dominio. et habebat foris urbem. II. carucatas terrae sine geldo. Modo habet rex in dominio. et sunt ibi. CII. burgenses manentes. Wastae sunt uero. C. XI. mansiones. Ad hanc uillam pertinent XX. acrae prati. et LX. acrae siluae minutae. et XI. piscariae. Una ex his. est Berengarii de todeni. T. R. E. inter regem et comitem. ualuit. XVIII. libras. modo. XXX. libras.

Queen Edith had this small town of TORKSEY and the manor of Hardwick adjacent to it in lordship and she had 2 carucates of land without tax outside the town. Now the King has them in lordship; there are 102 burgesses who live there, but 111 messuages are unoccupied. To this town belong 20 acres of meadow, 60 acres of underwood and 11 fisheries. One of them is Berengar of Tosny's. Value before 1066, between the King and the Earl, £18; now £30.

In Harduic habuerunt Suen et Godric. I. carucatam terrae. et ibi manebant. XII. homines. Modo habet Rogerius de busli medietatem. et episcopus Lincoliensis aliam medietatem. ad Sanctam MARIAM de Stou. Ibi cadit tercia pars geldi regis. quod rex non habet de illa quae pertinet ad Dorchesyg.

In Hardwick Swein [* son of Svavi *] and Godric had 1 carucate of land; 12 men lived there. Now Roger of Bully has half and the Bishop of Lincoln the other half at St Mary's of Stow. There falls to [Hardwick] the third part of the King's tax which the King does not have from that [part] which is due from TORKSEY.

Manerium In CHERCHEBI. habuit comes Morcar. V. carucatas terrae ad geldum. Terra ad IIII. carucas. Nunc habet Rex ibi. I. carucam. et XIIII. sochemannos cum II. carucis et. V. uillanos et V. bordarios. cum. I. caruca. Ibi dimidia aecclesia. T. R. E. ualuit. IIII. libras. modo VIII. libras cum pondere et arsione.

[* KESTEVEN *] `ASWARDHURN' Wapentake ?M? In KIRKBY[-la-Thorpe] Earl Morcar had 5 carucates of land taxable. Land for 4 ploughs. Now the King has 1 plough there. 14 Freemen [with 2] ploughs [and] 5 villagers and 5 smallholders with 1 plough. 1/2 church. Value before 1066 £4; now £8 weighed and assayed. [= ?? Land of the King. King William holds it =]

Berewita Euedune iacet in supradicto Manerio. X bouatae terrae ad geldum. Terra ad. I. carucam. Ibi. II. sochemanni et II. uillani habent. I. carucam. Ibi molendinum. V. solidorum et IIII. denariorum. et sedes. I. molendini. et VI. acrae prati.

?B? EVEDON lies in the [lands of the] above manor. 10 bovates of land taxable. Land for 1 plough. 2 Freemen [and] 2 villagers have 1 plough. A mill, 5s 4d; 1 mill site; meadow, 6 acres. ^[Outlier of Kirkby[-la-Thorpe]. Earl Morcar had it]^ [= ?? Land of the King. King William holds it =]

Ad eundem Manerium pertinet haec soca. Oustorp. I. cucarata. Welle. II. bouatae et dimidia. Hechintone. I. bouata. . Cornintone. I. bouata Simul ad geldum. I. carucata et dimidia. et dimidia bouata. Terra ad. I. carucam et dimidiam. In his habet rex. VII. sochemannos. habentes. I. carucam. Ibi. I. molendinum. XII. solidorum. et XIII. acrae prati. et XI. acrae siluae minute. et XX. acrae maresch.

Also to this manor belongs this jurisdiction: AUSTHORPE, 1 carucate; HOWELL, 2 1/2 bovates; HECKINGTON, 1 bovate; QUARRINGTON, 1 bovate. Altogether, 1 1/2 carucates and 1/2 bovate taxable. Land for 1 1/2 ploughs. In these [lands] the King has 7 Freemen who have 1 plough. 1 mill, 12s; meadow, 13 acres; underwood, 11 acres; marsh, 20 acres. ^[Jurisdiction of Kirkby[-la-Thorpe]. Earl Morcar had it]^ [= ?? Land of the King. King William holds it =]

Manerium In CHIME. habuit Morcar comes. IIII. carucatas terrae et II. bouatas ad geldum. Terra ad. II. carucas. Nunc habet Rex ibi dimidiam carucam. et XII. uillanos et III. bordarios cum. II. carucis. Ibi. II. aecclesiae et I. presbyter. et II. acrae prati. et VI. piscine. IIII. or solidorum. Silua pastillis per loca. CC. et X. acrae. et septies. C. acrae maresc. T. R. E. ualuit XL. solidos. modo. LX. solidos cum pondere et arsione.

?M? In [South] KYME Earl Morcar had 4 carucates of land and 2 bovates taxable. Land for 2 ploughs. Now the King has 1/2 plough there. 12 villagers and 3 smallholders with 2 ploughs. 2 churches and 1 priest; meadow, 2 acres; 6 fishponds, 4s; pasturable woodland in [various] places 210 acres; marsh, 700 acres. Value before 1066, 40s; now 60s weighed and assayed. [= ?? Land of the King. King William holds it =]

Manerium In BODEBI habuit Morcar comes. VIII. carucatas terrae ad geldum. Terra ad. IX. carucas. Rex habet ibi. II. carucas. et XX. uillanos et II. bordarios. habentes. IIII. carucas. et XI. sochemanni de. III. carucatis. habentes. III. carucas. Ibi aecclesia et presbyter. una carucata terrae iacet in aecclesia. Ibi. I. molendinum III. solidorum. et CXX. acrae prati. T. R. E. ualuit. XX. libras. modo similiter XX. libras. cum pensione.

[* BOOTHBY Wapentake *] ?M? In BOOTHBY [Graffoe] Earl Morcar had 8 carucates of land taxable. Land for 9 ploughs. The King has 2 ploughs there. 20 villagers and 2 smallholders who have 4 ploughs; 11 Freemen with 3 carucates who have 3 ploughs. A church and a priest; 1 carucate of land lies in the church [lands]. 1 mill, 3s; meadow, 120 acres. Value before 1066 £20; now the same, £20 weighed. [= ?? Land of the King. King William holds it =]

Manerium In WELLINGOURE. habuit Morcar. XVIII. carucatas terrae ad geldum. Terra ad totidem carucas. Rex habet nunc ibi. II. carucas. et VII. uillanos et VII. bordarios. cum. I. caruca. et XXVIII. sochemannos cum. VII. carucis. Ibi aecclesia et presbyter. habens. II. carucatas et II. bouatas de eadem terra. et Ipsa aecclesia pertinet ad aecclesiam Sacti Petri in Lincolia. Ibi. CXXIX. acrae prati. et aliae XIIII. acrae pertinent ad ecclesiam T. R. E. ualuit. XXX. libras. modo XV. libras cum pensione.

?M? In WELLINGORE [* Earl *] Morcar had 18 carucates of land taxable. Land for as many ploughs. The King now has 2 ploughs there; 7 villagers and 7 smallholders with 1 plough; 28 Freemen with 7 ploughs. A church and a priest, who has 2 carucates and 2 bovates of this land. This church belongs to St Peter's Church in Lincoln. Meadow, 129 acres; another 14 acres belong to the Church. Value before 1066 £30; now £15 weighed. [= ?? Land of the King. King William holds it =]

Manerium In COLEBI. habuit Siuuard. VII. carucatas terrae ad geldum. Terra ad totidem carucas. Rex habet nunc ibi. I. carucam. et V. uillanos et VI. bordarios cum. I. caruca. et X. sochemannos cum. II. carucas. et XXX. acras prati. T. R. E. ualuit. IIII. libras. Rex uero. Willelmus. apposuit hanc terram in Washingeburg. et ibi appreciatur. Presbyter et aecclesia. habens. I.

?M? In COLEBY Siward had 7 carucates of land taxable. Land for as many ploughs. The King now has 1 plough there. 5 villagers and 6 smallholders with 1 plough; 10 Freemen with 2 ploughs. Meadow, 30 acres. Value before 1066 £4; but King William placed this land in Washingborough; it is valued there. A priest and a church, which has 1 bovate of this land. [= ?? Land of the King. King William holds it =]

Soca et Berewita In ipsa Colebi sunt XII. carucatae terrae ad geldum. Terra ad totidem carucas. Ex his. est una caruca inland in Washingeburg. undecim uero sunt in soca. Hanc terram habuit Radulfus stalre. Nunc habet rex ibi XIIII. sochemannos et VII. uillanos cum. VX. VIII. carucis. et LX. acras prati. Huius precium. est in Washingeburg.

?S&B? In COLEBY itself there are 12 carucates of land taxable. Land for as many ploughs. 1 carucate of these is {inland}, in Washingborough, but 11 are in a jurisdiction. ^[Earl]^ Ralph the constable had this land. Now the King has there 14 Freemen and 7 villagers with 8 ploughs; and meadow, 60 acres. Its valuation is [included] in Washingborough. [= ?? Land of the King. King William holds it =]

Manerium In GRANTHAM. habuit Edid regina. XII. carucatas ad geldum. Non. est ibi terra arabilis extra uillam. Ibi non habuit quisque sacam et socam. praeter Elsuid monialem. quae dedit Sancto Petro de burg et hoc habet modo Colegrim. cum saca et soca. Regina Eddid habuit aulam. et II. carucatas. et terram de. III. carucis. sine geldo. Burgenses. C. et XI. Toftes sochemanniorum teignorum quater XX. III. minus. Nunc similiter habet rex. Iuo inuenit. I. carucam. et adhuc est. et LXXII. bordarios. AEcclesiam. I. cum. VIII. toftis. et IIII. molendina de XII. solidis. et VIII. acras prati sine geldo. De superioribus toftis. calumniatur episcopus dunelmensis. VII. toftas. quas habet Ernuinus presbyter. et hundret testimonium portat episcopo. T. R. E. tota Granham fuit soca ad. LII. libras. modo reddit. C. libras ad pondus. AEcclesia fuit tunc ad. VIII. libras. modo est ad. X. libras. sed non ualet nisi. C. solidos

[* `WINNIBRIGGS' Wapentake *] ?M? In GRANTHAM Queen Edith had 12 carucates taxable. No arable land outside the town. No one had full jurisdiction except the nun Alswith, who gave it to St Peter's of Peterborough. Kolgrim [* of Grantham *] has it now, with full jurisdiction. Queen Edith had a hall, 2 ploughs and land for 3 ploughs, without tax. 111 burgesses [and?] 80, less 3, plots of the Freemen of the thanes. Now the King has the same. Ivo [* Tallboys *] found 1 plough - it is still there - and 72 smallholders and 1 church with 8 plots; 4 mills at 12s; meadow, 8 acres, without tax. Of the above plots the Bishop of Durham claims 7 plots, which Ernwin the priest has; the hundred bears witness for the Bishop. Before 1066 the whole of Grantham was a jurisdiction, at £52; it now pays £100 by weight. The church was then [valued] at £8; now it is at £10, but the value is only 100s. [= ?? Land of the King. King William holds it =]

Soca et Berewita In GUNFordebi. sunt VII. carucatae terre ad geldum. Terra ad. IX. carucas. Tres carucatae sunt inland. et IIII. soca in Grandham. Ibi habet rex modo. I. carucam. et XXI. sochemannos. et I. uillanum et I. bordarium. habentes. VI. carucas. et C et V. acrae prati.

?S&B? In GONERBY 7 carucates of land taxable. Land for 9 ploughs. 3 carucates are {inland} and 4 [are] a jurisdiction of Grantham. The King now has 1 plough. 21 Freemen, 1 villager and 1 smallholder who have 6 ploughs. Meadow, 105 acres. ^[Queen Edith had it]^ [= ?? Land of the King. King William holds it =]

Soca et Berewita In HERLAUESTUNE. sunt XII. carucatae terre ad geldum. Terra ad. XVI. carucas. Nouem sunt in soca. et III. in aula Granham. Rex habet ibi nunc X. uillanos. et II. bordarios. cum. II. carucis. et LVIII. sochemannos. habentes. XIIII. carucas. Ibi. II. molendina. II. solidorum et LX. acrae prati. et LX. spineti.

?S&B? In HARLAXTON 12 carucates of land taxable. Land for 16 ploughs. 9 [carucates] are in a jurisdiction and 3 [are] in the hall of Grantham. The King now has 10 villagers and 2 smallholders with 2 ploughs; 58 Freemen who have 14 ploughs. 2 mills, 2s; meadow, 60 acres; spinney, 60 [acres]. ^[Queen Edith had it]^ [= ?? Land of the King. King William holds it =]

Soca In Sudstoches VI. et Nortstoches. III. sunt. IX. carucatae terrae ad geldum. Terra ad. XII. carucas. Nunc habet rex ibi. I. carucam. et XVI. uillanos. cum. III. carucis. et X. sochemannos cum. II. carucis. et II. molendina reddentia XXI. solidos et IIII. denarios. Prati. IX. quarentenas. et III. lati. et XX. acras.

?S? In `SOUTH STOKE' [* STOKE [Rochford] *] 6 and `NORTH STOKE' 3 - 9 carucates of land taxable. Land for 12 ploughs. Now the King has 1 plough there. 16 villagers with 3 ploughs; 10 Freemen with 2 ploughs. 2 mills which pay 21s 4d; meadow, 9 furlongs long and 3 wide and 20 acres. ^[Jurisdiction of Grantham. Queen Edith had it]^ [= ?? Land of the King. King William holds it =]

Soca In Nongtone. sunt. III. carucatae terre et V. bouatae |et dimidia ad geldum. Terra ad tantundem. Soca in Granham. Ibi habet rex. XIII. sochemannos. et VI. uillanos. cum. IIII. carucis. et I. molendinum XIII. solidorum et IIII denariorum. et III. acras prati.

?S? In {NONGTONE} 3 carucates of land and 5 1/2 bovates taxable. Land for as much. A jurisdiction of Grantham. The King has 13 Freemen and 6 villagers with 4 ploughs. 1 mill, 13s 4d; meadow, 3 acres. ^[Queen Edith had it]^ [= ?? Land of the King. King William holds it =]

Manerium et Soca In magna PANPTUNE. habuit Edid regina. XII. carucatae terre ad geldum. Terra ad. X. carucas. Nunc. est haec terra soca in Granham. Ibi sunt modo. X. uillani et III. bordarii. et XII. sochemanni. et dimidia caruca. Tantum inuenit Iuo ibi. Silua minuta. VIII. quarentenas longa. et III. lata.

?M&S? In GREAT PONTON Queen Edith had 12 carucates of land taxable. Land for 10 ploughs. This land is now a jurisdiction of Grantham. Now 10 villagers, 3 smallholders, 12 Freemen and 1/2 plough. Underwood 8 furlongs long and 3 wide. So much Ivo [* Tallboys *] found there. [= ?? Land of the King. King William holds it =]
In Summerdebi. habet rex. VIII. sochemannos cum. II. carucis /et dimidia.
In [Old] SOMERBY the King has {{2 1/2 carucates and 2 bovates}}. 8 Freemen with 2 1/2 ploughs. ^[Jurisdiction of Grantham. Queen Edith had it]^ [= ?? Land of the King. King William holds it =]

In Langetona. sunt. III. bouatae terre ad geldum. Terra ad VI. boues Soca. est in Stratone. et LXV. acrae siluae minutae ibi.

In LANGTON[-by-Wragby] 3 bovates of land taxable. Land for 6 oxen. It is a jurisdiction of `[Little] Sturton'. Underwood, 65 acres there. ^[Godwin and Gunnhvati had it]^ [= ?3? Land of the Bishop of Durham =]
Soca et Berewita In Wispinctune. II. carucatae terrae ad geldum. Terra ad. IIII. or carucas. Inland et Soca. in Stratone et Cherchebi. Ibi IX. sochemanni et VI. bordarii. cum. III. carucis. f

[* HORNCASTLE Wapentake *] ?S&B? In WISPINGTON 2 carucates of land taxable. Land for 4 ploughs. {Inland} and a jurisdiction of `[Little] Sturton' and Kirkby[-on-Bain]. 9 Freemen and 6 smallholders with 3 ploughs. [* Godwin and Gunnhvati had it *] [= ?3? Land of the Bishop of Durham =]
Soca In Waldingurde. VI. carucatae terre ad geldum. Terra ad. VI. carucas. Soca. est in Stratone et Cherchebi. Ibi XX. sochemanni cum IIII. carucis. et XX. acrae prati. et XXV. acrae siluae minutae

?S? In WADDINGWORTH 6 carucates of land taxable. Land for 6 ploughs. It is a jurisdiction of `[Little] Sturton' and of Kirkby[-on-Bain]. 20 Freemen with 4 ploughs. Meadow, 20 acres; underwood, 25 acres. ^[Godwin and Gunnhvati had it]^ [= ?3? Land of the Bishop of Durham =]

Manerium In CHIRCHEBI. habuit Haroldus. X. bouatas terrae ad geldum. Terra ad XIII. boues. Ibi habet episcopus dunelmensis. III. uillanos et II. bordarios cum dimidia caruca. et XII. acrae prati. et CLXX. acrae siluae pastilis per loca. T. R. E. ualuit C. solidos. modo similiter.

?M? In KIRKBY[-on-Bain] Harold [* brother of Aelfric and Guthfrithr *] had 10 bovates of land taxable. Land for 13 oxen. The Bishop of Durham has 3 villagers and 2 smallholders with 1/2 plough. Meadow, 12 acres; pasturable woodland in [various] places, 170 acres. Value before 1066, 100s; now the same. [= ?3? Land of the Bishop of Durham =]

In Martone. est soca huius Manerii. XII. bouatae ad geldum. Terra ad. II. carucas. Ibi. III. sochemanni et II. bordarii habent. I. carucam. et XXX. IIII. acrae prati. et XXX. III. acrae silue pastilis. et LX. acrae siluae minutae.

In MARTIN there is a jurisdiction of this manor; 12 bovates taxable. Land for 2 ploughs. 3 Freemen and 2 smallholders have 1 plough. Meadow, 34 acres; pasturable woodland, 33 acres; underwood, 60 acres. ^[Jurisdiction of Kirkby[-on-Bain]. Harold [* brother of Aelfric and Guthfrithr *] had it]^ [= ?3? Land of the Bishop of Durham =]

In Torp. est inland et soca eiusdem Manerii. II. carucatae terre ad geldum. Terra ad. II. carucas. Ibi XVI. uillani et I. sochemannus et IIII. bordarii habent. I. carucam. et terciam partem. II. molendinorum. de. VIII. solidis. et III. K piscarias de. VII. solidis et VI. denariis. et XV. acrae prati. et CXX. acrae siluae pastilis. Eudo calumniatur.

In [Tattershall] THORPE there is {inland} and a jurisdiction also of this manor; 2 carucates of land taxable. Land for 2 ploughs. 16 villagers, 1 Freeman and 4 smallholders have 1 plough and ?k? the third part of 2 mills at 8s; 3 fisheries at 7s 6d; meadow, 15 acres; pasturable woodland, 120 acres. Eudo [* son of Spirewic *] claims it. ^[{Inland} and jurisdiction of Kirkby[-on-Bain]. Harold [* brother of Aelfric and Guthfrithr *] had it]^ [= ?3? Land of the Bishop of Durham =]

Manerium In COUENHAM. habuit Esbern. II. carucatas terre et dimidiam ad geldum. Terra ad. III. carucas et VI. boues. Nunc |de episcopo habet Sanctus Karilefus ibi. II. carucas. et dimidiam. et XII. uillanos. et VI. sochemannos cum. I. caruca et dimidia. Ibi aecclesia. et LX. acrae prati. et II. saline. III. solidorum. T. R. E. ualuit. LX. solidos $ modo. IIII. libras. Tailla. XX. solidos.

[* LINDSEY NORTH RIDING *] [* LUDBOROUGH Wapentake *] ?M? In COVENHAM Esbiorn had 2 1/2 carucates of land taxable. Land for 3 ploughs and 6 oxen. Now [* the Abbey of *] Saint-Calais has 2 1/2 ploughs there from the Bishop. 12 villagers and 6 Freemen with 1 1/2 ploughs. A church; meadow, 60 acres; 2 salt-houses, 3s. Value before 1066, 60s; now £4. Exactions 20s. [= ?3? Land of the Bishop of Durham =]
$ In Scitebroc est inland de Couenham. VII. bouatae terre ad geldum. Terra ad. IX. boues. Ibi Sanctus Karilefus habet. VI. homines cum. II. carucis. et IX. acras prati.

[* In LINDSEY SOUTH RIDING *] [* In `LOUTHESK' Wapentake *] In `SKIDBROOKE' there is {inland} of Covenham; 7 bovates of land taxable. Land for 9 oxen. [* the Abbey of *] Saint-Calais has 6 men with 2 ploughs and meadow, 9 acres. ^[Esbiorn had it]^ [= ?3? Land of the Bishop of Durham =]
In Germundtorp. est soca de Couenham. IIII. bouatae terre et dimidia /ad geldum. Ibi. VI. uillani habent. I. carucam et II. boues.

`LOUTHESK' Wapentake In GRAINTHORPE there is a jurisdiction of Covenham; 4 1/2 bovates of land taxable. 6 villagers have 1 plough and 2 oxen. Meadow, 50 acres. ^[Esbiorn had it. [* the Abbey of *] Saint-Calais has it]^ [= ?3? Land of the Bishop of Durham =]
In Grimesbi. est soca huius Manerii. II. bouatae terre ad geldum. Terra ad. III. boues. Wasta fuit. modo colitur.

[* LINDSEY NORTH RIDING *] [* LUDBOROUGH Wapentake *] In [Little] GRIMSBY there is a jurisdiction of this manor, 2 bovates of land taxable. Land for 3 oxen. It was waste; it is now cultivated. ^[Jurisdiction of Covenham. Esbiorn had it. [* the Abbey of *] Saint-Calais has it]^ [= ?3? Land of the Bishop of Durham =]

. II. Maneria In FOREBI. habuerunt Summerled et Archil VII. bouatas ad geldum. Terra ad. II. carucas et V. boues. Ibi Turstinus homo episcopi habet. I. sochemannum. VI. uillanos habentes. I. carucam. et XLII. acrae prati. T. R. E. ualuit. IIII. libras. modo. XX. solidos.

?2M? In FOTHERBY Sumarlithi [* son of Karli *] and Arnketil had 7 bovates taxable. Land for 2 ploughs and 5 oxen. {Turstin}, the Bishop's man, has 1 Freeman; 6 villagers who have 1 plough. Meadow, 42 acres. Value before 1066 £4; now 20s. [= ?3? Land of the Bishop of Durham =]

Manerium In ENDREBI et Radebi habuit Elnod. IIII. bouatas et dimidiam ad geldum. Terra ad. V. boues. Nunc. est dimidia caruca ibi. et I. uillanus. et dimidia sedes molendini. et X. acrae prati. T. R. E. ualuit. X. solidos. modo similiter.

[* LINDSEY SOUTH RIDING *] [* BOLINGBROKE Wapentake *] ?M? In [Mavis] ENDERBY and RAITHBY[-by-Spilsby] Alnoth had 4 1/2 bovates taxable. Land for 5 oxen. Now 1/2 plough there. 1 villager. 1/2 mill site; meadow, 10 acres. Value before 1066, 10s; now the same. [= ?3? Land of the Bishop of Durham =]

Manerium In SPILESBI et Iresbi et Torp. habuit Aschil. VI. carucatas terre ad geldum. Terra ad. VI. carucas. Ibi habet episcopus. I. carucam. et V. uillanos et V. sochemannos. et I. bordarium. habentes. I. carucam. et II. molendinum. IX K solidorum. et XII. acrae prati. T. R. E. ualuit. XX. solidos. modo similiter. Eudo /clamat.

?M? In SPILSBY, ERESBY and THORPE [St Peter] Eskil had 6 carucates of land taxable. Land for 6 ploughs. The Bishop has 1 plough. 5 villagers, 5 Freemen and 1 smallholder who have 1 plough. 2 mills, 9s; meadow, 12 acres. Value before 1066, 20s; now the same. ?k? Eudo ^[son of Spirewic]^ claims it. [= ?3? Land of the Bishop of Durham =]
Ibidem. est soca. de Gredbi. et Estrecale. II. carucatae terrae ad geldum. Terra ad. III. carucas. Ibi XII. sochemanni habent. II. carucas.

There also ^[SPILSBY, ERESBY and THORPE [St Peter]]^ is a jurisdiction of Grebby and East Keal; 2 carucates of land taxable. Land for 3 ploughs. 12 Freemen have 2 ploughs. [= ?3? Land of the Bishop of Durham =]

Manerium In ESTRECALE. habuit Elnod. VI. bouatas terre ad geldum. Terra ad. VI. boues. Ibi. II. uillani arant. II. bobus. Ibi. V. acrae K prati. T. R. E. ualuit X. solidos. modo similiter. Eudo clamat socam.

?M? In EAST KEAL Alnoth had 6 bovates of land taxable. Land for 6 oxen. 2 villagers plough with 2 oxen. Meadow, 5 acres. Value before 1066, 10s; now the same. ?k? Eudo ^[son of Spirewic]^ claims the jurisdiction. [= ?3? Land of the Bishop of Durham =]
In Totintune. est soca de Spilesbi. II. bouatae terre ad geldum. Terra ad. II. boues. Ibi. I. sochemannus. et VI. acrae prati.
In TOYNTON there is a jurisdiction of Spilsby; 2 bovates of land taxable. Land for 2 oxen. 1 Freeman. Meadow, 6 acres. ^[Eskil had it]^ [= ?3? Land of the Bishop of Durham =]
